item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Basal Cell Carcinoma
item.description: Get your weird growths checked no matter how small!! What was once a little speck that had been growing slowly over the 1st 10 years and has been doubling in size for the last 7ish was removed yesterday. I’d seen 3 different dr’s over the years to ask what the hell it was and each of them said “it was nothing to worry about.” Finally during Covid my current dr. noticed it and sent me to an ophthalmologist. Ophthalmologist decided to do a biopsy and I found out it was Basal Cell Carcinoma and had to be removed. That dr couldn’t remove it himself as it involved my tear duct and I was sent to see 2 different specialists. 1 to remove the cancer and an oculoplastic surgeon rebuild it. I was awake for both procedures. 1st day was a Moh’s procedure. Basically they freeze the area and then cut away the cancer and then send to pathology while I wait. They check the margins and if there’s still cancer cells they cut a little more until it’s all gone. 2nd reconstruction procedure was a lot more involved and really intense. The surgeon who performed the procedure is apparently the best in western Canada so I’m super hopeful that my eye lids will still function properly and won’t look too messed up. TL:DR skin cancer on your eyelid is brutal, get your little growths checked!
item.dc:creator: FattsoCattso

item.title: Over a year ago, I posted this stupid meme when it all started. Today, we launched the book.
item.description:
Old meme is cringe, long live new meme.
Today we released the PDF version and we're shipping the physical book from next week. Shameless plug, you can order a copy from our website if you want: https://palaeogames.com/ Take a look at some of our art, if you'd like:
These illustrations are by Mark Witton. He's a pretty cool guy. This is Balaur - watch out for its dual sickle claws.
One of our custom playable races, the Manyhorn. Specifically, a Barbarian, Conan style!
We added a bunch of holistic environments too. These are Beipiaosaurus in the chilly mountains of the Yixian Formation. Who said dinosaurs can only be in Aztec-themed jungles?
It has been a very strange year for me. I have the same hair style as Michael here too.
item.dc:creator: Palaeo

and hair

mmm hmm
that's all for now.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Bought and paid for it to make it untrustworthy
item.dc:creator: CannonBallNuts

item.title: Normalize personal growth for the sake of growth
item.description: So, around three years ago, I decided to follow up on a desire of mine to learn a second language. I picked Swedish. I don't have much rationale behind it, beyond the fact that it's relatively close to English and I wanted to learn it. So, I did it. I'm not perfectly fluent but I can hold a fairly basic conversation and read it somewhat well. I still practice; watching Swedish shows/movies, reading Dagens Nyheter and other Swedish newspapers, etc. However, whenever I bring up the fact that I know Swedish, people always seem confused as to why I picked it. I know it's none a common second language in America like Spanish or a popular world language (also like Spanish or) like Mandarin but I chose to learn it and find myself always having to answer questions about why I want to. People also seem confused when I know topics about science or history and don't have degrees in the matter. I don't think it should be odd to be well-versed in topics and not necessarily pursue an education in it, beyond one conducted on your own.
item.dc:creator: LincLoud

Also I have seen reindeer so I know they're real.
no.
mood
six chins

:3
Oh no.
You think that's bad? Scots Gaelic has "Saor" which means "Free" as in Speech (Saor Alba) and "Cheap" (Leann Saor). Free (as in Beer) is "An Asgaidh".
What do you think Chat.
You will use the rotary dial.
OwO
This is what sleeping is like for me ;__;

item.title: The cutest old man in the world, Kirby
item.description:
This is Kirby, my best friend. Kirby is 8 years old now! As a veterinary student, it is my life goal to make his life as enjoyable and as long as possible. I'd be lost without him. He is the perfect study buddy. As you may notice, Kirby did not approve of his winter shoes.
He has spunk and will tell you how he feels
Blep
Such a dapper gentleman
He knows how to bow
The picture of refinement
Kirby has a healthy respect for cats. Bug secretly loves him
The puppy days!
A young Kirby, very sassy
Who needs personal space?
I call this his army cut. Basically I just trim the scruff up during the summer
Liv the cat doesn't approve of dogs. Thanks for looking at my dog Kirby, the cutest dog in the entire world, just like all dogs.

item.title: The Giant Alaskan Malamute is a selectively bred Malamute that exceeds 70 kg in weight and 70 cm of height, originally bred to pull heavy goods between cities throughout Alaska. They have been recorded to pull loads up to 500 kg…
